What happened this week:
  - Paradis' Playmakers won Robinson, Brian (RB, SFO) on waivers.
  - Italian Cavallini added Myers, Jason (PK, SEA) (free agent).
  - Eddie & the Cruisers added Chargers, Los Angeles (Def, LAC) (free agent).
  - Stu's Crew added Burrow, Joe (QB, CIN) (free agent).
  - Paradis' Playmakers added Mafah, Phil (RB, DAL) (free agent).
  - Paradis' Playmakers added Corum, Blake (RB, LAR) (free agent).
  - Weichert's Warmongers added Ravens, Baltimore (Def, BAL) (free agent).
  - Italian Cavallini added Falcons, Atlanta (Def, ATL) (free agent).
  - Robb's Raiders added Pollard, Tony (RB, TEN) (free agent).
  - Robb's Raiders added Lions, Detroit (Def, DET) (free agent).

--- SHAREABLE RECAP ---
Robb exploded for 137.90 points to demolish Brandon 47.80 in the week's biggest blowout, extending Brandon's losing streak to 10 games. De'Von Achane carried the Raiders with 37.50 points while Brandon managed just 90.10 — his second-lowest output of the season. The winless campaign continues.

KP's Playmakers stayed atop the standings with a 137.50-103.10 win over Italian Cavallini, powered by Jonathan Taylor's season-high 48.10 points. That performance topped the previous season best by over a point and helped the Playmakers bounce back from last week's loss to maintain their one-game lead. Michele left 59.20 points on the bench, including 17.70 from Tyler Allgeier, but even optimal lineup decisions wouldn't have flipped this result.

Steve's three-game win streak continued with a 130.80-118.10 victory over Ben's Gods. Jahmyr Gibbs delivered 36.70 points for the Warmongers while Ben got 37.70 from Matthew Stafford but couldn't overcome Garrett Wilson's zero. The loss dropped Ben to 3-7, and he's now left 15-plus bench points on the table in seven different weeks this season.

Miller squeaked past Eddie & the Cruisers 107.40-103.55 in the week's closest game, decided by just 3.85 points. Josh Allen's third straight 25-plus point performance (26.40 this week) carried Miller while Eddie got 20.00 from the Seattle defense but left a painful 70.90 points on the bench. Caleb Williams scored 29.30 on Eddie's bench — enough to flip the result. Eddie is now 15-10 in games decided by fewer than five points, the best clutch record in the league, but this one slipped away.

Stu snapped Purple Haze's seven-game win streak with a 112.70-93.40 victory behind J.J. McCarthy's 19.20 points. Pat's offense sputtered as A.J. Brown managed just 2.30 points, and he left 35.60 optimal points on the bench. The loss dropped Purple Haze into a tie atop the standings with the Playmakers at 8-2.
--- END SHAREABLE RECAP ---
